title: Hattie's Magic Mouse Summoning
summary: Hattie the mouse discovers that the whimsical words she invents are actually powerful magic spells. By chanting 'Baskamadasca,' she accidentally summons her friend Mischief Mouse from a roof-patching job and later calls upon the giant Pinecone Mouse. When the group faces a mysterious problem, the Super Sleuths—Darby, Pooh, and Tigger—zip in on their scooters to investigate. They quickly deduce that the real issue is a collective case of the rumbles, leading to a grand feast of honey and cheese at Pooh Bear's house.

highlightQuote.text: Keep telling the story and then you'll find out!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What are you talking about?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Daska daska daska for you! It's yours!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, a made-up word for me?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, turn off that lamp. That's always about Hattie. Always about Hattie, always.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, always about Hattie. Alright. It's time for Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Every night. Every night.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Are you going to be in the chair for Stars and Stories? Can I sit with you or should I sit on the little stool?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: You can sit on the line stool.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Once upon a time, Hattie the mouse was walking down a path through the forest near her house and she was thinking about words. And she thought to herself, maybe I'll make up a word. Okay, can you make up the word? What's Hattie's word?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Baskamadasca, jiggidy jaska.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And she thought to herself, baskamadasca, jiggidy jaska. And suddenly there was a poof! Because she didn't know it, but that word that she just made up happened to be a magic spell. And it was the kind of spell that summons a creature. Summoning is like, bringing it to you. And so what kind of a creature did the spell summon? Was it a tiny sparkly fairy, or a big elephant-like creature that was an unexpected color? Or...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was Mischief Mousey. It was Mischief Mousey.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The creature was Mischief Mouse. Well, there was a poof after Hattie the mouse said her strange made-up word that turned out to be a magic spell. And then tada! There was Mischief Mouse looking confused. 'How did I get here?' she said. And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We just walked along.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We just walked along.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, well I was at my house, actually I was working up on my roof, patching a little hole in my roof, which is why I have this hammer in my hand, but just a minute ago I was on my roof and then I came here. And Hattie said, 'Well, it must be because I said a magic spell that poofed you right here.' And Mischief said, 'Well, um that's pretty amazing. I wonder what other kind of magic we could do.' And so they sat together in the woods and thought, 'Hmm, maybe if we made up just the right words, we could summon someone else. Is there some friend of theirs that they haven't seen in a very long time that they would like to magically bring to them?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Pinecone! Baskamadasca, jiggidy jeeza!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Hattie said her new spell, baskamadasca, jiggidy jeeza, and then poof! There was a poof and a pop and a little rustle down on the forest floor because Pinecone Mouse was actually a very small mouse.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, he was a very big mouse. He was a size as a wall. He was this tall!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He was so tall! Pinecone Mouse was so tall. Was he taller than Hattie Mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, he could almost reach the ceiling of our house.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He could almost reach the ceiling! I didn't realize Pinecone Mouse was so big. Well, there was a poof. And Hattie the mouse and Mischief Mouse said, 'Wow, Pinecone Mouse, we missed you so much! So glad to see you again.' And Pinecone Mouse said, 'Um, what am I doing here?' And they said, 'Pinecone, are you okay?' And Pinecone Mouse said, 'Yeah, I was just taking a nap at home so I'm still a little groggy.' And Mischief and Hattie said, 'Oh sorry, well, would you like to come over for tea?' And Pinecone Mouse said, 'Well, you know...' maybe you could help me with a problem that I have. And Hattie said, 'Oh yeah, maybe we could. We know a new magic spell, maybe it will help.' And Pinecone told them the problem that he had. What was Pinecone Mouse's problem? Was it...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the story and then we'll find out!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so, was it that Pinecone Mouse was looking for...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, keep telling the story and then you'll find out!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'm trying! Was Pinecone Mouse looking for a pet for his house, like a cat or a dog? Or was Pinecone Mouse looking for someone to dance with? Or was Pinecone Mouse looking...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Keep telling the story!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I want you to help me come up with what it's about. I need your help. So Pinecone Mouse said, 'I have a problem. I need someone...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Or maybe the Super Sleuths can help you! Just sound the siren!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: How does the siren, what does the siren sound like? Is it like wee-ooo wee-ooo or woop-woop?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, it's wee-ooo wee-ooo!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So they sounded the siren and the Super Sleuths came. And what did they see?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Darby, Pooh, and Tigger.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Darby, Pooh, and Tigger ran as fast as they could along the forest floor...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, they drove on their scooter! Vroom!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, they drove on their scooters. And they saw Mischief Mouse and Hattie Mouse and Pinecone Mouse. Um, and they were all old friends, they hadn't seen each other in a long time. And Darby said, 'Well, what seems to be the problem? The Super Sleuths are here!' And Hattie said, 'Well, I learned how to do a magic spell and I summoned Mischief Mouse and then we summoned Pinecone Mouse. But Pinecone Mouse has a problem, but we don't even know what the problem is that Pinecone Mouse has.' And Darby and Tigger and... who's the other one?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Pooh! Pooh Bear!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Pooh Bear. They all put their heads together and they thought and they thought and they thought and they said, 'Well, if there's a problem but Pinecone Mouse doesn't know what it is...' and Pooh Bear suddenly said, 'Aha! I know what the problem was.' And Tigger said, 'Oh, I think I know what you know the problem was. I think we all know what Pooh Bear's problem always is.' But Pooh says, 'Oh no no, it's everyone's problem. Everyone always just wants a delicious little snack of honey.' And Pinecone Mouse said, 'Now that you mention it, I was a little bit hungry for a snack.' And Mischief Mouse and Hattie Mouse said, 'Well, all this casting of important spells has made us a little bit hungry. But what can we do about it, Super Sleuths?' And Darby turned back to her friends Tigger and Pooh and said, 'What can we do about it?' And Tigger says, 'Well, we could go to the grocery store and buy some honey.' And Pooh Bear said, 'Oh no, no, no!'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Cheese! Cheese! Some cheese and honey would be great!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Cheese and honey! And so Pooh Bear invited them all back to his house where he happened to have a cabinet full of cheese and a big jar full of honey. And Hattie Mouse and Mischief Mouse and Pinecone Mouse and Darby and Pooh Bear and Tigger all sat around a big table snacking until they were all full. The end.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, that's the end of Stars and Stories.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, could you do a little shadow puppets?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you mean shadow puppets?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Just a little bit. Woof woof woof! You want to try? If you stand up right here and then hold out your hand, and then look straight up while you hold up your hand. There it is, you see it? And then if you hold it lower towards the light it gets bigger. Try that. So look up but lower your hand. Like that. And stick your fingers out. There you go! Oh, that was nice. You made a little bunny rabbit kind of like this with the two ears. Crunch, crunch, crunch.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Munch munch munch munch munch!

characters.name: Hattie the Mouse
characters.description: A nimble, friendly mouse of Possible Street and loyal friend to Izzy and Seeker, this brave artisan is the wife of Dimitar and mistress of the Workshop of Hats—a weathered hollow tree sanctuary on Cornell Street scented with cedar, ink, and peanut butter toast. Clad in her signature red cloak and pointy hat, she is a resourceful hatmaker whose spirit was forged against massive diggers and the rain-slicked Floofer Cloud. Within her paint-stained home, she carefully curates her husband’s library alongside her own hand-painted volumes while confronting a baffling supply mystery; the discovery of curious clover stickers signals a gathering shadow of intrigue she is determined to unravel.

characters.name: Mischief Mouse
characters.description: Reigning from a purple-striped Jovian castle, this affable mouse King—a playful-named companion to Pinecone Mouse—is a seasoned adventurer, guardian of the Underground Library, and Hattie’s steadfast ally. Between savoring artisanal cheeses and awaiting a rugged cardboard replacement for his fragile Lego hat, he spends leisure hours meticulously constructing and gleefully smashing soaring towers. A pancake-loving monarch who once tended shop for mountain-dwelling traffic cones, his cosmic quests recently shifted from the stars to the speedway; having borrowed a tow truck from Mistress Mouse, he proved a formidable competitor who nearly clinched a stunning victory. Whether piloting starships or joining the group’s latest trek to Nana’s house, he remains a vigilant protector and devoted friend across the galaxy.

characters.name: Pinecone Mouse
characters.description: As tall as a garden wall and driven by an insatiable hunger, this colossal mouse dwells within a tiny, precarious house perched among the highest needles of a swaying pine tree.

characters.name: Darby
characters.description: One of the Super Sleuths who arrives on a scooter to solve problems.

characters.name: Pooh Bear
characters.description: A honey-loving Super Sleuth whose likeness is woven into the game upon the wall, renowned for his singular insight that the group’s direst problems are often rooted in nothing more than a collective, gnawing hunger.

characters.name: Tigger
characters.description: A friendly and boisterous Super Sleuth from the Hundred Acre Wood, this bouncing adventurer zips into action on a scooter, bringing a whirlwind of enthusiasm to help his friends and aid the local mice.

places.name: The Forest
places.description: Cloaking the surface of the green planet in a vast mantle of dense greenery, this whispering woodland serves as the lush setting of the 'Forest Friends' chronicle. It is a realm where creatures play eternal hide-and-seek between Hattie’s cozy clinic and the ancestral cradle of Finn’s companions. Along magical trails where Hattie and the Pakruder Worm harvest hat-making ingredients, winding paths veil secret gopher tunnels and hidden mole cottages, forming the essential route toward the wizard's castle. While its sun-drenched threshold remains a sanctuary for the Rainbow Giraffe and Mama Bird, the deeper, darker thickets harbor a somber mystery; it was within these sprawling, uncharted depths—the same eerie expanse where the sick creature was first spotted and Mossy was discovered—that Mole recently vanished into the shadows.

places.name: Mischief Mouse's House
places.description: Atop the weathered rafters where Mischief’s roof repairs were cut short by her sudden summoning, a festive transformation has taken hold; here, Mistress Mouse presides over a steaming spaghetti feast, her boisterous family gathered amidst the shingles and moonlight to celebrate where labor was left behind.

places.name: Pooh Bear's House
places.description: The cozy residence of Winnie the Pooh, a sun-drenched haven where friends gather for indulgent feasts of savory cheese and golden honey, and where Pooh and Piglet sit in patient silence, waiting for the gentle arrival of a new idea.
